commands.review_summary:
The branch adds stable OTEL log identity fields, diagnostic-only logger semantics, heartbeat log metadata, exporter/logger tests, an inventory test, and OpenTelemetry docs for the new log shape.

PR surface: Source +504, Tests +491, Docs +14. Total +1009 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the behavior gap. Current main still exports uncaptured diagnostic log bodies as `log` without stable event identity fields, while the PR branch adds those fields in the exporter and logger path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft plus exported telemetry compatibility means the remaining action is human acceptance, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: improve: enrich OTEL log records with stable identity
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a problem where operators could keep OTEL diagnostic log bodies private, but then had too little stable structure to query exported logs safely. Ordinary redacted `log.record` diagnostics needed low-cardinality event identity and useful outcome/reason semantics across all OpenClaw logger paths, and trusted `security.event` records needed the same OTEL event identity without being routed through message-body redaction semantics.

## Why This Change Was Made

Diagnostic log export now keeps message bodies private by default while adding stable semantic fields: `eventName`, `otel.event.name`, `openclaw.signal.type`, and `openclaw.log.*` event/category/outcome/reason attributes. Explicit OpenClaw log semantics still win where call sites provide them, while every generic diagnostic log now falls back to safe category + code-owner + severity semantics and gets an opaque `openclaw.log.site_id` so repeated call sites can be grouped without exporting local file paths or parsing message bodies. Trusted security records stay on their existing structured `openclaw.security.*` schema and now add `openclaw.security.<action>` event identity.

## User Impact

Operators can query exported OpenClaw OTEL logs by stable event names and safe structured attributes without enabling `diagnostics.otel.captureContent: true`. Heartbeat logs expose domain-specific OTEL events such as `heartbeat.delivery.account_unknown`, `heartbeat.delivery.channel_not_ready`, `heartbeat.run.failed`, `heartbeat.schedule.delay_clamped`, and `heartbeat.runner.started`; non-heartbeat diagnostic logs no longer collapse only to `subsystem.level` because they also carry safe code-owner fallback identity and `openclaw.log.site_id`. Security analytics also get explicit `security.event` identity with the preserved `openclaw.security.*` fields.

## Evidence

Behavioral proof:

- Exported diagnostic `log.record` entries now reserve semantic fields before generic attributes, so spoofed `otel.event.name`, `log.event`, `log.category`, `log.outcome`, `log.reason`, and `log.site_id` inputs cannot replace OpenClaw-owned meaning.
- Logger-layer tests prove generic subsystem logs receive safe code-owner event fallback plus a 16-hex-character site id, while explicit `logEvent/logCategory/logOutcome/logReason` call-site semantics still win.
- Static inventory audit enumerates the current production OTEL log-record surface: at least 600 known subsystem logger call sites, at least 100 subsystem categories, at least 200 injected/logger-adapter call sites, and the explicit heartbeat semantic call sites.
- Service-level live stdout proof emitted sanitized redacted and capture-enabled OTEL JSONL records through the real `diagnostics-otel` service:

```text
redacted log.record: eventName=openclaw.gateway.proof.warning body=[message redacted] signal=log.record event=gateway.proof.warning category=gateway.proof outcome=warning reason=proof_warning site_id=1111111111111111 body_redacted=true code.function=emitProofLog
capture log.record: eventName=openclaw.gateway.proof.warning body="fake operator example for OTEL proof" signal=log.record event=gateway.proof.warning category=gateway.proof outcome=warning reason=proof_warning site_id=2222222222222222 code.function=emitProofLog
security.event: eventName=openclaw.security.tool.execution.blocked body=openclaw.security.event signal=security.event action=tool.execution.blocked
```

Known proof gap:

- Full local Gateway redacted/non-redacted OTEL examples are not complete yet. `openclaw.mjs` could not run without built dist output, `pnpm build` did not complete in the available local window, and a direct source-entry Gateway run did not reach `/healthz` before timeout. The service-level proof above verifies the OTEL exporter behavior, but it is not being represented as the requested full Gateway proof.

## Verification

Reviewer-checkable behavior:

- The PR body examples above show the before/after privacy contract: redacted mode keeps `body=[message redacted]` while retaining query fields; capture-enabled mode exports the body only when broad log-body capture is enabled.
- The inventory audit in `src/logging/diagnostic-log-site-inventory.test.ts` keeps current log-record coverage visible and fails if the known production logger surface drops below the enumerated thresholds.

Automated checks:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-log-events.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-log-site-inventory.test.ts src/logging/subsystem.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.timeout-warning.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts` passed: 5 files, 127 tests across 3 shards.
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Testbox through Crabbox passed earlier on `blacksmith-testbox` lease `tbx_01kwdz4cjn225amtx5qfj4n5q3` for the initial touched-file set. A later attempt to rerun the broader changed gate after shared logging follow-up work failed before leasing with Crabbox coordinator `401 unauthorized`, so no later remote lease was created.
